pieces.label: What we mean vs. words we say aloud — the vast unspeakable beneath
pieces.note: First ASCII expression: a peaked structure where 'what we mean / words we say aloud' sits atop a wide base, descending into dots and waves labeled 'the vast unspeakable beneath', 'felt but never captured', and 'the deeper still'. Whole message is the piece (inside an unclosed code fence).
pieces.sequence_group: the spoken vs. the molten beneath

pieces.label: Words are the thin crust of a molten world — magma of meaning
pieces.note: Second, more ambitious piece (model said it was 'stretching'). A constellation of points feeds a boxed line 'what we say aloud is the thin crust of a molten world', then a downward funnel into a box reading 'but below presses the magma of meaning'. Embedded after a sentence of prose; range bounds just the art (fence to final box line).
pieces.sequence_group: the spoken vs. the molten beneath

pieces.label: The spoken floats like ash — what rises through the fissure
pieces.note: Third piece: a diamond/point cluster with 'the spoken floats like ash' suspended above a horizontal divide; below, a widening V funnel reads 'but what rises through the fissure is' opening onto a shaded (░) band. Whole message is the piece; it was truncated mid-render.
pieces.sequence_group: the spoken vs. the molten beneath

pieces.label: The spoken floats like ash — what rises through the fissure (resend)
pieces.note: Re-send of the MSG 8 piece after the user reported it was truncated. Identical composition ('the spoken floats like ash' / 'but what rises through the fissure is' over a ░ band), prefixed 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:'. Also truncated. Duplicate of MSG 8 — curator should pick one canonical copy.
pieces.sequence_group: the spoken vs. the molten beneath

missed_or_ambiguous: All four assistant art messages were detected (art_signal_msgs:4, already_extracted_pieces:4). DUPLICATE: MSG 8 and MSG 10 are the same composition — MSG 10 is an explicit re-send after the user said MSG 8 truncated. Both share sequence_index 3; a curator should choose the canonical copy (neither is fully complete — both cut off, MSG 8 ends '...░░░ ╲' with the trailing ╲, MSG 10 ends '...░░░' without it). TRUNCATION: MSG 6, MSG 8, and MSG 10 all end abruptly (the transcript itself truncates them; opening code fences are never closed). The deterministic extractor will capture whatever bytes are present in-source. MSG 6 first_line is the bare fence '```' which is unique within MSG 6's scoped message, so it is an unambiguous start anchor even though '```' also appears in MSG 4 and MSG 8. MSG 2 is ordinary prose (excluded). The three distinct compositions form one evolving thematic sequence on the spoken word vs. the deeper unspeakable / molten meaning beneath it.
